nameMeanings.name: Hinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Hinton (for example, in Shropshire, Herefordshire, Gloucestershire, Somerset, Hampshire, and Northamptonshire). Some of the placenames, such as those in Northamptonshire, Shropshire, and Herefordshire, derive from Old English hīna, genitive plural form of hīwan ‘household, religious community’, + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’ (compare Hine as the first element). Others, such as those in Gloucestershire, Somerset, and Hampshire, derive from Old English hēan, dative form of hēah ‘high’ + tūn.

variants.name: Hine
meanings: English (Devon): occupational name from Middle English, Old English hīne ‘servant, member of a household’, also ‘farm laborer’ (such as a herdsman or shepherd). Originally a collective term for a body of servants, from an Old English plural noun, hīwan ‘household’, in early modern English it commonly acquired excrescent -d (see Hinde ). Evidence from Nottinghamshire shows that some bearers of the name were wealthy freemen, which suggests that their eponymous ancestors were not farmhands but senior members of a lord's household. From the late 15th century onward in western England, from south to north, a hine or hind also denoted a farm steward or manager, a sense that may be relevant to the surname in northwestern England, where hereditary naming had not been fully established by the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Hinton was born in 1550, in Eastwell, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sr Thomas Hinton, was 40 and her mother, Anne Goddard, was 38. She had at least 2 sons with Thomas Howse. She died about 1590, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Hinton est née en 1550, à Eastwell, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sr Thomas Hinton, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Anne Goddard, avait 38 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Thomas Howse. Elle est décédée environ 1590, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Hinton 1550 in Eastwell, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sr Thomas Hinton 40 und ihre Mutter Anne Goddard 38. Sie hatte mit Thomas Howse mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb um 1590 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Hinton è nata il nel 1550 nel Eastwell, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sr Thomas Hinton, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Anne Goddard, aveva 38 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Thomas Howse. Morì circa 1590, nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 41 anni.
